message:
fix(list): apply offset after client-side filtering for correct pagination

When client-side filters (desc_contains, notes_contains, priority_min/max,
id list, deferred, overdue) are active, the SQL query fetches all matching
issues without LIMIT/OFFSET, then Rust-side filtering narrows the result
set. The offset was not applied after this filtering, so paginated requests
with client filters always returned results from the beginning of the
filtered set regardless of the --offset value.

Fix: take offset out of the SQL filters alongside limit when client_filters
is true, then apply the offset in Rust after client-side filtering but
before truncation. This ensures the pagination envelope metadata (offset,
has_more) matches the actual returned issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
